text:
*[Bug 19070] Support \leq, \geq and \neq as aliases of \le, \ge and \ne in Rd2txt*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19070>

Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
             Status|UNCONFIRMED                 |CLOSED
         Resolution|---                         |FIXED
                 CC|                            |seb.meyer@fau.de

--- Comment #1 from Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) ---
Thank you for the report. I have a vague recollection that I have stumbled over
documentation "typos" such as `for N >=q 2` before. Indeed, comparing text
conversions of ~23k CRAN package Rd DBs before and after adding \leq and \geq
substitution shows that ~800 packages are affected: these use the
single-argument form of \eqn (or \deqn) without providing an "ASCII" version
for plain-text (and legacy HTML) help.

r90031 now substitutes \leq and \geq in addition to \le and \ge, avoiding the
stray "q". I intend to port this change to the R-4-6-branch. These basic
substitutions in plain-text help are now also documented in WRE.

\ne and \neq were not previously substituted, but I agree it makes sense to
handle them too, simplifying other short equations like `for i \ne j` or `if x
\neq 0`. These appear less frequently than `leq?` and `geq?` but still quite
often. This was added in r90033.


PS: it is on my TODO/Wishlist to make use of Unicode symbols for these Rd2txt
substitutions (in UTF-8 locales) and also for emdash and some others.

text:
*[Bug 19055] grDevices::embedFonts does not embed all fonts*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19055>

--- Comment #3 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Created attachment 3696
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3696&action=edit>
documentation patch

Here's the documentation patch and the two examples, with and without the
command to reset the `NeverEmbed` list.

```
plotname <- "test.pdf"
grDevices::pdf(plotname, encoding='ISOLatin1.enc')
plot(0, type="n", main=quote(paste(Delta, "pCO"[2], " [µatm]")))
dev.off()
grDevices::embedFonts(plotname, outfile=plotname)
system(paste('pdffonts', plotname))
# name                                 type              encoding         emb
sub uni object ID
# ------------------------------------ ----------------- ---------------- ---
--- --- ---------
# Helvetica                            Type 1            Custom           no 
no  no       9  0
# Symbol                               Type 1            Custom           no 
no  no      10  0

grDevices::pdf(plotname, encoding='ISOLatin1.enc')
plot(0, type="n", main=quote(paste(Delta, "pCO"[2], " [µatm]")))
dev.off()
grDevices::embedFonts(plotname, outfile=plotname, options = c("-c",
shQuote("<</NeverEmbed []>> setdistillerparams"), "-f"))
system(paste('pdffonts', plotname))
# name                                 type              encoding         emb
sub uni object ID
# ------------------------------------ ----------------- ---------------- ---
--- --- ---------
# AICJQG+Helvetica                     Type 1C           Custom           yes
yes no       9  0
# MWCXZR+Symbol                        Type 1C           Custom           yes
yes no      11  0
```

Here's the documentation patch and the two examples, with and without the
command to reset the

plotname <- "test.pdf"

sub uni object ID

--- --- ---------

no  no       9  0
# Symbol                               Type 1            Custom           no 
no  no      10  0

grDevices::pdf(plotname, encoding='ISOLatin1.enc')
plot(0, type="n", main=quote(paste(Delta, "pCO"[2], " [µatm]")))
dev.off()
grDevices::embedFonts(plotname, outfile=plotname, options = c("-c",
shQuote("<</NeverEmbed []>> setdistillerparams"), "-f"))
system(paste('pdffonts', plotname))
# name                                 type              encoding         emb
sub uni object ID
# ------------------------------------ ----------------- ---------------- ---
--- --- ---------
# AICJQG+Helvetica                     Type 1C           Custom           yes
yes no       9  0
# MWCXZR+Symbol                        Type 1C           Custom           yes
yes no      11  0

text:
*[Bug 18035] a bug in wilcox.test() caused by precision error when there are ties*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18035>

--- Comment #6 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
I went through all CRAN packages with 'wilcox.test' and found only two are
sensitive to `digits.rank` ({ggpubr} and {TOSTER} fail at `digits.rank=5L` and
`digits.rank=3L`, respectively).

So, `digits.rank=10L` wouldn't break any CRAN packages; OTOH, it might still be
nice to motivate the choice with a formal citation.

#### CRAN packages with wilcox.test

```
gh search code '"wilcox.test"' \
  --owner cran --language R --limit 1000 \
  --json repository --jq '.[].repository.nameWithOwner' \
  | sort -u | awk -F"/" '{print $2}' | tr "\n" ", "
armada,cardx,caTools,clintools,CoFRA,cogmapr,CRMetrics,EasyDescribe,effectsize,EnvStats,ExomeCNV,exprso,eyetrackingR,fgac,geneHapR,ggpubr,ggpval,ggsignif,gtsummary,iCellR,iDOS,jsmodule,lazyWeave,LGDtoolkit,MetaIntegrator,microeco,mnda,mt,PairedData,pairwiseCI,PALMO,papeR,pctax,pcutils,PLEXI,plotbb,plotthis,pmg,PopComm,qPCRtools,RadOnc,ratPASTA,rattle,rbiom,Rcmdr,RcmdrPlugin.EACSPIR,RcmdrPlugin.MPAStats,rcompanion,rempsyc,ReporterScore,SCpubr,Shrinkage,sigminer,Statomica,tinyarray,TOmicsVis,TOSTER,traitr,UCSCXenaShiny,voiceR,volcano3D
# NB: must be intersect()'d with available.packages() to exclude those off-CRAN
```

#### `wilcox.test` patch to set `digits.rank=` from `env`

```
Index: src/library/stats/R/wilcox.test.R
===================================================================
--- src/library/stats/R/wilcox.test.R   (revision 89975)
+++ src/library/stats/R/wilcox.test.R   (working copy)
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
 function(x, y = NULL, alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),
          mu = 0, paired = FALSE, exact = NULL, correct = TRUE,
          <http://conf.int|conf.int> = FALSE, conf.level = 0.95, tol.root = 1e-4,
-         digits.rank = Inf, ...)
+         digits.rank = as.numeric(Sys.getenv("WILCOX_DIGITS_RANK", "Inf")),
...)
 {
     alternative <- match.arg(alternative)
     if(!missing(mu) && ((length(mu) > 1L) || !is.finite(mu)))
```

#### Binary search to find the minimum digits.rank that passes their `R CMD
check`

<https://gist.github.com/MichaelChirico/11b1a273dbf14ba44739aa52f60cd4da>

#### Script for generating two packages for confirming the script works

Basically, we observe the following:

```
wilcox.test(c(1.11, 2.22), c(1.14, 2.25), digits.rank=2L)$statistic[["W"]]
# [1] 2
wilcox.test(c(1.11, 2.22), c(1.14, 2.25), digits.rank=3L)$statistic[["W"]]
# [1] 1
wilcox.test(c(1.11111, 2.22222), c(1.11114, 2.22225),
digits.rank=5)$statistic[["W"]]
# [1] 2
wilcox.test(c(1.11111, 2.22222), c(1.11114, 2.22225),
digits.rank=6)$statistic[["W"]]
# [1] 1
```

<https://gist.github.com/MichaelChirico/e74dadfeeedbec9ca9dd28f3b0ec1f85>

#### Result

| Package | Minimum valid `digits.rank=` |
| ------- | ---------------------------- |
| ggpubr  | 5 |
| TOSTER  | 3 |
| 43 other packages | 1 |

_assisted by Gemini_

text:
*[Bug 18952] Add option to specify IP address used by help.start() and tools::startDynamicHelp()*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18952>

Simon Urbanek (<mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
         Resolution|---                         |WONTFIX
             Status|TRIAGED                     |CLOSED

--- Comment #4 from Simon Urbanek (<mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>) ---
Thanks. As noted, this is not a general-purpose service and thus the comparison
to those is not directly relevant, so in light of the discussion I'll close
this.
